build.description: Build a Twick package (or all packages if no name). Usage: /build [package-name]
build.template:
Run the Twick build for a specific package or for the whole monorepo.

If the user provided a package name (e.g. `timeline`, `studio`, `effects`, `cloud-transcript`), use the matching `pnpm build:<name>` script from the root `package.json` (see CONTRIBUTING.md). If the name doesn't match an existing build script, fall back to `pnpm turbo run build --filter=@twick/<name>`.

If no package name was given, run `pnpm build` to build all packages.

Report the build status clearly. If it fails, surface the first error and suggest a fix (check the style guide, missing peer dep, or stale `dist/` — try `pnpm clean:packages` then rebuild).

Remember: do NOT push or commit. Build only.

dev.description: Start the dev server for a Twick package in watch mode. Usage: /dev [package-name]
dev.template:
Start a Twick package in development/watch mode.

If a package name is given (e.g. `studio`, `canvas`, `timeline`, `examples`), use the matching `pnpm dev:<name>` script from the root `package.json`. If the name doesn't match, use `pnpm turbo run dev --filter=@twick/<name>`.

If no name is given, ask which package the user wants to develop. Prefer `dev:examples` (it demos the studio) for a quick visual check, or `dev:studio` for the main package work.

Reminders:
- These are long-running watch processes. Tell the user how to stop them (Ctrl+C).
- `dev:examples` is the hosted demo; it usually serves on a local port (Vite default).
- Do NOT combine multiple `dev:*` tasks; they conflict on ports.

test.description: Run tests for a Twick package (or all if no name). Usage: /test [package-name]
test.template:
Run the Twick test suite.

If a package name is given, use `pnpm turbo run test --filter=@twick/<name>`. If no name, use `pnpm test` (runs all).

For the cloud-export-video smoke test, use `pnpm test:smoke`.

Report which tests passed/failed with their counts. If a test fails, surface the assertion message and the most likely cause (recent change to the relevant package; check the git diff for that package).

lint.description: Lint the codebase (or one package). Usage: /lint [package-name] [--fix]
lint.template:
Lint the Twick codebase.

If a package name is given, scope the lint to that package using `pnpm turbo run lint --filter=@twick/<name>`. If no name, run `pnpm lint` for everything.

If the user passed `--fix` (or says "fix"), run `pnpm lint:fix` (or `pnpm turbo run lint --fix --filter=@twick/<name>` for one package).

Twick's lint config lives in each package's `eslint.config.*` and the root devDeps include `eslint@^9.25.0` and `typescript-eslint@^8.30.1`. See STYLE_GUIDE.md for the conventions the linter enforces.

new-package.description: Scaffold a new @twick/* package following the Twick conventions. Usage: /new-package <kebab-name>
new-package.template:
Scaffold a new package in the Twick monorepo following the conventions in `CONTRIBUTING.md` §"Adding a New Package".

The user provides a kebab-case name (e.g. `my-feature`, `audio-effects`, `caption-exporter`).

Steps:
1. Confirm the name with the user if it's ambiguous (e.g. is it `audio-effects` or `effects-audio`?).
2. Read `CONTRIBUTING.md` §"Adding a New Package" for the exact template.
3. Read an existing leaf package for reference (e.g. `packages/media-utils/`) to copy the conventions.
4. Create `packages/<name>/` with:
   - `package.json` named `@twick/<camel-or-kebab-name>` (check existing package names for the convention — most are single words: `studio`, `canvas`, `timeline`; multi-word use kebab: `browser-render`, `live-player`).
   - `tsconfig.json` extending the root base config.
   - `vite.config.ts` mirroring an existing package.
   - `src/index.ts` re-exporting the public API (empty placeholder is fine).
   - `.eslintrc` or `eslint.config.*` matching the convention in the nearest sibling package (e.g. `packages/media-utils/`).
5. Add a `build:<name>` and `dev:<name>` script to the root `package.json` (use the existing pattern: `turbo run build --filter=@twick/<name>`).
6. Verify it builds: `pnpm build:<name>`.
7. Report what was created and any choices you made.

Do NOT add the package to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` — the wildcards already cover `packages/*`.

Do NOT publish, commit, or push.

sync-upstream.description: Sync this fork with the upstream ncounterspecialist/twick. Usage: /sync-upstream
sync-upstream.template:
Sync the user's Twick fork (`thedanw/twick`) with the upstream (`ncounterspecialist/twick`).

Workflow (load the `twick-sync-upstream` skill first for the full procedure):

1. `git fetch upstream`
2. `git status` — refuse to proceed if the working tree is dirty. Ask the user to commit, stash, or discard first.
3. `git checkout main`
4. `git merge --ff-only upstream/main` — fast-forward only. If it cannot fast-forward, the user has diverged; ask before doing anything else.
5. `git push origin main` — push the synced main to the fork.
6. Report the new HEAD SHA and how many commits were brought in.

Never force-push. Never rewrite history. If a fast-forward is not possible, stop and explain.

release.description: Prepare a Twick release: create a changeset entry, then version + publish. Usage: /release [patch|minor|major]
release.template:
Walk the user through a Twick release using changesets.

Load the `twick-publish` skill first.

1. Confirm the bump level (patch / minor / major). Default to minor if not specified. Check the recent `git log` for context.
2. Run `pnpm changeset` interactively (or with `-- --help` to show options). The user picks the affected packages and the bump level.
3. Show the generated `.changeset/<random>.md` file and ask the user to confirm the content.
4. Do NOT run `pnpm version` or `pnpm release` yet. The user will trigger those after review.
5. Remind the user: PR the changeset to `main` (or to their feature branch), get review, merge, then run `pnpm version` (which consumes the changeset and bumps versions) followed by `pnpm release` (which publishes to npm). The full release script is `turbo run build && changeset publish`.

See CONTRIBUTING.md and the root `package.json` `scripts.release` for the canonical flow.
